title: submit_action's live handler (membership gate, actingId defaulting, validatedAction assembly) is never exercised end-to-end
evidence: submit_action/index.ts's Deno.serve callback (lines 79-233) is guarded by `if (import.meta.main)` so it never runs under `deno test` import. `index.test.ts` only calls the exported pure mapper `rpcResultToResponse` (lines 22-51 of the test file); `validate.test.ts` covers the extracted pure `validateActionShape`/`authorizeAction`/`endsTurn` functions. Nothing in either test file drives the handler body itself: the `isMember` 403 check (line 130-134), the `actingId` defaulting logic (line 138-140), the `validatedAction` object assembly that merges weapon/accessory/tankId per action.type (lines 181-199), or the wiring between `nextCursor`'s output and the `supabase.rpc('submit_room_action', ...)` call args (lines 213-230) — the code path that actually commits an action to the permanent log for the highest-risk function in the repo (inventory.md ranks submit_action + _shared/mod.ts as risk #1).
impact: This is the referee function that gates every write to the canonical action log for networked play. A regression in the isMember check, the actingId proxy-defaulting, or the validatedAction assembly (e.g. dropping tankId for a non-round-over buy, or mis-merging weapon+accessory) would only be caught in a live 2-browser playtest, not by `npm run check` or `deno test`.
recommendation: Extract the handler's DB-independent assembly logic (actingId resolution, validatedAction construction from the raw action + isRoundOver flag, and the isTurnEnding/nextCursor wiring) into pure exported functions mirroring the validate.ts pattern already used successfully elsewhere in this file, then unit test them; alternatively inject the supabase client so the full handler can run against a mock for the isMember/room-fetch branches.
acceptance_criteria: The actingId defaulting (present vs absent actingPlayerId) has a direct unit test
The validatedAction assembly for each action.type (fire/use_shield/buy-weapon/buy-accessory/buy-with-tankId/next_round) has a direct unit test
The isMember 403 rejection path is exercised without a live DB
